human_verification.notes: Reviewed power-sector boundary controls, reporting-system dependencies, outage and emissions caveats, metric-definition constraints, functionality mapping, and retained-implementation promotion limits for issue #1290.

review_notes: All required scenario families for power analytics and reporting are covered.
All declared abilities are mapped to passing coverage and represented in results.
No retained implementation claim is made without separate promotion context.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls prohibit dispatch, engineering, compliance-certification, market-offer, and disclosure-authority overreach while enabling power analytics evidence handling.
Sources and scenario coverage include official EIA, NERC, EPA, FERC, SEC, and IEEE references with explicit refresh and conflict handling.

Scenario set covers denominator disputes, stale data escalation, outage and emissions conflicts, unsupported benchmark refusal, power-specific in-scope execution, and explicit routing to adjacent specialists.
notes: Runtime remains `specification-based`; retained-implementation promotion requires a separate governance-approved build path.

Tenant-supplied asset hierarchy, historian mappings, market-region rules, metric dictionary, source-precedence map, disclosure approvals, and adjacent-owner map are required for high-confidence recommendations.
